name: Pantano de la Palma de Ebro – circular por la cueva de Hospital Santa Llúcia
description: This moderate 18.6-mile mountain bike loop near La Palma d'Ebre features 1721 feet of climbing and passes the historic Santa Llúcia Hospital
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around La Palma D'Ebre are set within a diverse landscape characterized by rugged terrain, including parts of the Montsant Natural Park and the Priorat region. The area features varied elevations, from challenging ascents with gradients exceeding 10% to more verdant, lush sections. This provides a range of mountain biking experiences, with routes often traversing through natural features and offering viewpoints over the surrounding countryside. The terrain includes both demanding climbs and flowing paths suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around La Palma D'Ebre The most popular mountain bike trail is Tour of Montsant MTB Trail, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) difficult trail that takes 5 hours 1 minute to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the Montsant Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop, a moderate 26.1 miles (42.0 km) path. This route follows a long-distance path, often alongside the Ebre river, offering varied scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop, a 15.5 miles (25.0 km) trail leading through the rural landscapes of the Priorat region, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around La Palma D'Ebre is defined by rugged hills, natural parkland, and rural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in La Palma D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 18 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Palma D'Ebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in La Palma D'Ebre?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around La Palma D'Ebre, catering to various skill levels. This includes 7 easy routes, 19 moderate routes, and 7 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in La Palma D'Ebre?
A: The terrain in La Palma D'Ebre is diverse, featuring rugged hills, natural parkland, and rural paths. You'll find varied elevations, from challenging ascents with gradients exceeding 10% in areas like the Priorat region, to more verdant, lush sections within the Montsant Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in La Palma D'Ebre?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955890171/pantano-de-la-palma-de-ebro-circular-por-la-cueva-de-hospital-santa-llucia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de la Palma de Ebro – circular por la cueva de Hospital Santa Llúcia&lt;/a&gt; offers a 30 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946065325/la-granadella-bovera-palma-d-ebre-bellaguarda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a longer 41.4 km option.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see along the trails?
A: You can explore several interesting natural features and historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3224462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clot del Cirer&lt;/a&gt;, an oasis of greenery with year-round water, is located within the Montsant Natural Park. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5086443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/305718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escaladei Charterhouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in La Palma D'Ebre?
A: La Palma D'Ebre offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. The region's diverse landscape, including verdant areas even in drier seasons, suggests pleasant riding during spring and autumn. Summers can be warm, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944328028/tour-of-montsant-mtb-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Montsant MTB Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.3 km route with over 1100 meters of elevation gain through the Montsant Natural Park. The Priorat region, which borders La Palma D'Ebre, is also known for tough climbs with gradients exceeding 10%.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in La Palma D'Ebre?
A: The mountain bike trails in La Palma D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 18 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, which includes both demanding climbs and flowing paths, and the scenic beauty of the natural parkland and rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, the majority of trails are moderate. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944315935/gr-99-flix-and-riba-roja-d-ebre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 42 km path that often follows the Ebre river, offering varied scenery. Another local favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958887780/la-bisbal-de-falset-and-cabaces-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Bisbal de Falset and Cabacés Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 25 km route through the rural Priorat region.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink near the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific cafes or pubs directly on every trail are not listed, the region includes villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poboleda Village&lt;/a&gt;. These settlements often provide amenities where you can find refreshments after your ride.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3224462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clot del Cirer&lt;/a&gt; within the Montsant Natural Park is an emblematic spot known for having water present year-round, offering a refreshing stop. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e944315935/gr-99-flix-and-riba-roja-d-ebre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 99: Flix and Riba-roja d'Ebre Loop&lt;/a&gt; often follows the Ebre river, providing scenic views of the waterway.

Q: What kind of natural parks or protected areas do the trails traverse?
A: Many mountain bike trails in La Palma D'Ebre traverse parts of the Montsant Natural Park, known for its diverse landscapes and significant natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3224462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clot del Cirer&lt;/a&gt;. The region also borders the rugged Priorat area.
tips:
Located on the outskirts of the town of Palma d'Ebre in the direction of the reservoir, we find this impressive white pine, over 400 years old and with the largest trunk in all of Catalonia.

At their feet, a millstone, they serve as tables or chairs in case we want to recover energy with a snack.

If we take a hot day, the shade of this pine will be great.
Baroque church built in the 18th century. The building has an attached bell tower on the left side. Inside there are some decorative paintings.

Near the town of La Bisbal de Falset is the cave of Santa Llúcia.

During the Civil War, the space became the hospital for the military units made up of republican volunteers who fought in the Battle of the Ebro.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Pantano de la Palma de Ebro mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is categorized as a moderate mountain bike ride. It requires good physical fitness and advanced riding skills, corresponding to an STS (Single Track Scale) rating of approximately S2. Expect technical sections that demand competent bike handling.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain includes technical sections with obstacles like small roots and rocks, minor drops, and gradients up to 70%. Riders should be prepared for a mix of wider tracks and narrower singletrack sections, which may require shifting weight and precise braking. Some portions might even require dismounting and pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The most notable highlight is the Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave, which offers a unique blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll also pass by the Pine of la Palma d'Ebre and the Church of Sant Miquel de Margalef.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave accessible from the trail, and what's its significance?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail circles near the Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave. This immense cave, carved from conglomerate rock, is historically significant as it served as a field hospital for Republican troops during the Spanish Civil War in 1938. It was equipped with over 80 beds, an operating theatre, and a drugstore. Inside, there's also a natural spring, traditionally believed to cure eye problems.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't detailed, moderate trails in this region are generally best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder, potentially wet conditions, making the technical sections more challenging.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including the Camí de Sant Salvador, Camí del Pi de cal Pino, and Camí de la Bisbal de Falset, among others.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating and requirement for advanced riding skills (STS S2), this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It's best suited for experienced mountain bikers with good fitness.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Pantano de la Palma de Ebro trail?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify restrictions regarding dogs on this particular trail. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other trail users, and to follow local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that special permits or entrance fees are required to access or ride this trail. It appears to be open to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking areas or precise trailhead locations are not detailed in the available information. However, as it's a circular route starting near La Palma d'Ebre, it's likely you can find parking in or around the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural experience, the nearby village of La Palma d'Ebre and surrounding towns would be the best places to look for cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options before or after your ride.
